zh.lifeSummary: 當Janet Isabel Sinclair於1232年出生時，她的父親Henry Sinclair 5th Lord of Rosslyn18歲，母親Margaret Mar13歲。她於大約1250年與Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell在蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1271年2月1日在英國蘇格蘭易亞斯特 洛迪安登格拉斯享年39歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾可雷南 安德 達拉維其。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: Janet
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

variants.name: Jannet
meanings: Originally a diminutive of   Jane  , already in common use in the Middle English period. It remained in use in Scotland and in some parts of England well into the 17th century and was revived at the end of the 19th century to much more widespread use, while still retaining its popularity in Scotland. Since the 1960s, however, it has rather gone out of fashion in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

en.lifeSummary: When Janet Isabel Sinclair was born in 1232, her father, Henry Sinclair 5th Lord of Rosslyn, was 18 and her mother, Margaret Mar, was 13. She married Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell about 1250, in Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 1 February 1271, in Dunglass, East Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 39, and was buried in Kilchrenan and Dalavich, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Janet Isabel Sinclair est née en 1232, son père, Henry Sinclair 5th Lord of Rosslyn, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Margaret Mar, avait 13 ans. Elle a épousé Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell environ 1250, à Argyll, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 février 1271, à Dunglass, East Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterrée à Kilchrenan and Dalavich, Argyll, Écosse.

Lei sposato Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell circa 1250, nel Argyll, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 4 figlie. ja.lifeSummary: Janet Isabel Sinclairが1232年に生まれた時，父Henry Sinclair 5th Lord of Rosslynは18歳，母Margaret Marは13歳であった。約1250年にスコットランドアルグイルでCailean or Colin Mor Campbellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1271年2月1日にイギリススコットランドエアスト ロスアンダングラスで39歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルキルチレナン アンド ダラヴィチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Janet Isabel Sinclair(이)가 1232년에 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry Sinclair 5th Lord of Rosslyn(은)는 18세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Mar(은)는 13세였다. 그녀는 대략 1250년 스컷랜드 아그이에서 Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1271년 2월 1일 영국 스코틀랜드 이스트 로스이언 던글래스에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 킬크리난 앤드 다래비치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Janet Isabel Sinclair nasceu em 1232, seu pai, Henry Sinclair 5th Lord of Rosslyn, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Mar, tinha 13 anos. Ela casou-se com Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell aproximadamente 1250, em Argyll, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de fevereiro de 1271, em Dunglass, East Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 39 anos, e foi sepultada em Kilchrenan and Dalavich, Argyll, Scotland.

ru.Dunstaffnage, Scotland: рубха гарбх, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Janet Isabel Sinclair родилась в 1232 г., её отцу по имени Henry Sinclair 5th Lord of Rosslyn было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Mar, 13. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell около 1250 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 1 февраля 1271 г. в местности дунгласс, эаст лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 39 лет и была похоронена в местности килчренан анд далавич, аргилл, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Janet Isabel Sinclair nació en 1232, su papá, Henry Sinclair 5th Lord of Rosslyn, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Margaret Mar, tenía 13 años. Se casó con Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell aproximadamente 1250, en Argyll, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 1 de febrero de 1271, en Dunglass, East Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultada en Kilchrenan and Dalavich, Argyll, Scotland.
